items.correct: The zigzag movement of sediment along a coast caused by waves approaching at an angle (swash up the beach) and gravity returning water perpendicular (backwash).
items.misconception: Sand moves along the beach because tides pull it sideways.
items.random_wrong: Longshore drift is the rising of sea level over time.
items.why: Students confuse tides with waves. Longshore drift is a wave-driven process — the angle of swash vs straight-down backwash creates the zigzag, not tidal pull.

items.q: How do waves erode a cliff?
items.correct: Through hydraulic action (water compressing air in cracks), abrasion (waves throwing rocks against cliffs), attrition (rocks smashing against each other), and solution.
items.misconception: Waves erode cliffs by pushing water against the rock until it breaks.
items.random_wrong: Cliffs erode because of acid rain dissolving them.
items.why: Water pressure alone is rarely enough. The main mechanisms involve compressed air and rocks acting as tools — students underestimate how erosion needs material to do the wearing.

items.q: What is the difference between weather and climate?
items.correct: Weather is short-term atmospheric conditions at a specific time and place; climate is the long-term (typically 30-year) average of weather patterns in a region.
items.misconception: Weather happens outside and climate happens globally.
items.random_wrong: Weather refers to rain and climate refers to temperature.
items.why: Students treat them as different phenomena rather than different timescales. The key distinction is time — climate is statistical, weather is instantaneous.

items.q: What is the correct sequence of headland landforms?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Cave, arch, stack, stump. “Arch, cave, stump, stack” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Spurn Head, at the mouth of the Humber, is an example of which landform?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Spit. “Stack” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many landforms are in the cave-arch-stack-stump sequence?
items.why: The real figure is 4. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
